title: pavonia cracens
intro: Pavonia cracens, also known as the Red Rose Mallow, is a flowering plant species within the hibiscus family. Traditionally, it has been used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and alleviating respiratory issues, thanks to its rich content of flavonoids and mucilages. Herbal preparations of Pavonia cracens often involve infusions or decoctions of the plant's leaves and flowers, which can be consumed as teas or used topically in the form of creams or salves.
